paper_title: Long-term soil hydrological data of a Pleistocene region in North-East Germany

methods.description: This method involves measuring water and solute movement through the vadose zone, focusing on detecting seepage flow below the root zone. Regular measurements of soil water tension and content are conducted to understand soil hydrologic status and dynamics.

steps.description: Regular measurements of tensions with water filled pressure transducer tensiometers (accuracy 1 hPa) and water contents with TDR probes at 16 soil hydrological plots were carried out at several depths down to 3 m depth.
steps.searchable_summary: measure soil water tension and content using tensiometers and TDR probes

steps.description: The tension values were recorded in daily intervals.
The soil water content was measured in 3 day intervals with the TDR Easy test device.
steps.searchable_summary: measure soil water content every 3 days using TDR Easy test device

steps.description: Precipitation was measured at 1.10 m above the ground using a headed and wind-shielded gauge (type HP 3) with a sampling area of 200 cm2 coupled with a see-saw technique according to Joss-Tognini with a read contact (resolution: 0.1 mm).
steps.searchable_summary: measure precipitation using a wind-shielded gauge

steps.description: Temperature, relative air humidity, global radiation and some other meteorological variables were measured by the automatic micro-meteorological station FMA 86.
steps.searchable_summary: measure meteorological variables using micro-meteorological station

steps.description: The nitrate concentration of the seepage water was analysed in the laboratory of the ZALF.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze nitrate concentration in seepage water

steps.description: Taking deep seepage dynamics into consideration, nitrogen losses were estimated.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate nitrogen losses considering seepage dynamics
